title: Rash
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: Constrains the exact UAE evidence to pediatric telehealth.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

red_flags.text: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.
red_flags.rationale: Supports fever, systemic symptoms, rapid or persistent concern, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty as clinician prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

assessment.text: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.
assessment.rationale: Supports rash, itching, and fever grouping without diagnosing a cause.
assessment.evidence_extract: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

medication.text: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.
medication.rationale: Supports distribution, morphology, evolution, symptoms, exposures, medicines, travel, allergy, and visual assessment fields for children.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

source_limitations: Adult, pregnancy-associated, purpuric, medicine-associated, blistering, infectious, and lesion-specific rash pathways remain incompletely sourced.
Skin swab, blood tests, skin-care advice, trigger avoidance, fixed follow-up timing, and all diagnostic conclusions remain unsupported.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
